free_text: Neck emission of intermediate-mass fragments in the
fragment detector telescopes consisted of a gas
ionization chamber operated at 32 torr of CF4, a
500-microm passivated silicon detector of area 5 cm x
5 cm, and a CsI(Tl) crystal of thickness of 2 cm with
The solid angle was 10.3 msr and the
flight path was 30 cm in each case. These devices
provided charge resolution for Z =1-15 fragments,
MeV/nucleon. In addition, the timing signal from the
silicon detector and that from the cyclotron rf
permitted time-of-flight mass identification with a
timing resolution of about 300 ps. Both Z and A
identification were obtained for Z <= 4 fragments;
for Z >= 5 only charge identification was possible
due to low statistics. All spectra are in coincidence
No details on sources of uncertainties.
